title: Fixing CSS Media Query Bugs: Incorrect Media Feature Ordering

statement.markdown: You are given a CSS media query string as input. The media query string can contain multiple media features (e.g., width, height, orientation) and their corresponding values. Your task is to fix the bug in the given media query string by rearranging the media features to ensure that they are ordered correctly for the CSS parser to understand.
statement.inputFormat: A CSS media query string as a string input, e.g., `(max-width: 600px) and (min-height: 300px) and (orientation: landscape)`
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS media query string as a string output, e.g., `(min-height: 300px) and (max-width: 600px) and (orientation: landscape)`
statement.notes: Note that the media features can be in any order in the input string, and the output string should have the media features in the correct order for the CSS parser to understand.

constraints.description: The number of media features in the input string

examples.explanation: The media features are ordered correctly for the CSS parser to understand.
The media features are already in the correct order, so the output is the same as the input.

hints.text: The CSS parser understands media features in a specific order. You can use this information to reorder the media features correctly.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Media Query Bugs: Incorrect Media Feature Ordering
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to understand the order in which the CSS parser understands media features. By rearranging the media features according to this order, you can fix the bug in the given media query string.
editorial.bruteForce: You can try all possible permutations of the media features and check if the output is correct. However, this approach can be time-consuming and may not be efficient for large input strings.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a data structure like a stack or a queue to store the media features and then pop them off in the correct order.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a sorting algorithm to sort the media features in the correct order. For example, you can use the `sort()` method in JavaScript or the `sorted()` function in Python.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected media query string will pass the CSS parser and will be parsed correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Example: Input `(max-width: 600px) and (min-height: 300px) and (orientation: landscape)`. Output `(min-height: 300px) and (max-width: 600px) and (orientation: landscape)`. The media features are ordered correctly for the CSS parser to understand.
commonMistakes: Not understanding the order in which the CSS parser understands media features
Not rearranging the media features correctly
